engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Koleos Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Koleos in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Koleos M9R engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF blockage - soot accumulation causes warning lights and reduced power on short-journey vehicles.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts exhaust flow, triggering limp mode on high-mileage units.
Turbocharger wear - bearing failure above 100k miles leads to oil consumption and loss of boost.

cards.altText: Renault Koleos M4R engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - chain stretch causes rattling noise and potential valve damage after 80k miles.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear leads to higher oil top-ups on higher-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Renault Koleos 2QR engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough idle common after 70k miles on city-driven vehicles.
Coolant leak at thermostat housing - plastic housing cracks with age, causing overheating risk.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Koleos Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Koleos across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-volume 2.0 dCi unit powering most UK Koleos; known for strong torque but DPF sensitivity on short journeys.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique dCi, Expression dCi, Privilege dCi, Signature dCi
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure on urban-driven vehicles causing warning lights and reduced performance
EGR valve carbon buildup leading to rough idle and limp mode activation after 80k miles
Turbocharger bearing wear above 100k miles resulting in oil consumption and boost loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 700 Renault Koleos engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Uprated 175hp variant for 4x4 models; enhanced cooling but same core failure patterns as 150hp units.
compatibleVariants: Privilege dCi 4x4, Signature dCi 4x4, Bose Edition dCi 4x4
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting and power loss on high-mileage examples
Injector seal degradation leading to fuel leaks and diesel smell in engine bay
Timing chain tensioner wear creating rattling noise on cold starts after 90k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 704 Renault Koleos engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Late Phase 1 update with improved emissions hardware; still susceptible to DPF issues without motorway use.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique dCi, Privilege dCi, Signature dCi, GT Line dCi
commonFailures: AdBlue system faults on Blue dCi variants triggering NOx warnings and reduced power modes
Swirl flap actuator failure causing uneven combustion and increased emissions readings
Oil cooler gasket leaks leading to coolant contamination on vehicles over 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 830 Renault Koleos engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Blue dCi
entries.history: Phase 2 Euro 6 compliant unit with enhanced aftertreatment; higher complexity increases repair costs.
compatibleVariants: GT Line dCi, Initiale Paris dCi, Signature dCi, Privilege dCi
commonFailures: SCR catalyst degradation causing persistent AdBlue warnings and MOT emission test failures
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss and potential head gasket stress on high-mileage units
Variable geometry turbo actuator sticking resulting in boost pressure errors and limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 868 Renault Koleos engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated 2.0L petrol offering smooth delivery but lower torque than diesel; popular with low-mileage owners.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise on cold starts and potential valve interference after 80k miles
Ignition coil failure leading to misfires, rough idle and increased fuel consumption on city-driven vehicles
Oil consumption increase from piston ring wear requiring frequent top-ups on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M4R 700 Renault Koleos engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Top-spec 2.5L V6 petrol for performance-oriented buyers; higher power but increased fuel consumption and repair costs.
commonFailures: Coolant leak at thermostat housing causing overheating risk and potential head gasket damage on aged units
Ignition system component failure leading to misfires and reduced power under load after 70k miles
Intake manifold carbon buildup affecting throttle response and fuel economy on stop-start driven vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2QR 700 Renault Koleos engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Koleos Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Koleos primarily uses M9R diesel and M4R/2QR petrol engines, with DPF/EGR issues dominating diesel replacement enquiries and timing chain concerns affecting petrol units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair costs versus full replacement for your specific failure.

problems.h4: DPF Blockage & Regeneration Failure - Why Koleos Diesel Engines Struggle on Short Journeys
problems.affectedModels: Renault Koleos Dynamique dCi, Privilege dCi, Signature dCi, GT Line dCi (2008-2020, M9R 700/704/830/868)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent DPF reaching regeneration temperature, causing soot accumulation that triggers warning lights and reduced power modes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical clean or off-car regeneration to restore flow
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if substrate intact; may recur without driving pattern change

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished DPF unit fitted with gaskets and sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance; 50k+ mile lifespan with proper use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded components addressing DPF-related wear
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple systems affected; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Koleos has under 100k miles and the DPF is the only fault, a specialist clean or replacement offers better value. However, if you're also experiencing turbo wear, EGR issues or high oil consumption, a rebuilt M9R engine from our network often costs less than repairing multiple failing components individually.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R engine prices for your Renault Koleos

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - The Silent Power Killer on High-Mileage Koleos Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Renault Koleos all dCi variants (2008-2020, M9R series engines)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits over time, restricting flow and causing rough idle, limp mode, and failed emissions tests.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve removal, ultrasonic clean or legal blanking plate fit
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may trigger warning lights on newer Euro 6 models

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished EGR valve fitted with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Restores proper exhaust flow; 40k+ mile expected lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR system components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if EGR failure accompanied by turbo or injector issues

problems.recommendation: For isolated EGR faults on Koleos under 90k miles, specialist replacement offers the best balance of cost and longevity. If your vehicle has additional symptoms like turbo noise, injector knock or excessive oil consumption, compare quotes for a full rebuilt engine - it often proves more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M9R EGR repair or replacement on your Renault Koleos

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss and Oil Consumption on Koleos dCi Engines
problems.affectedModels: Renault Koleos all turbo diesel variants (2008-2020, M9R 700/704/830/868)
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing wear from oil starvation or carbon buildup causes shaft play, leading to boost loss, blue smoke, and potential engine damage from oil ingestion.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator replacement or calibration without full turbo removal
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for electronic fault codes; not mechanical bearing wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished turbo fitted with updated oil feed lines
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure; 60k+ mile lifespan with proper maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo and oil system components
repairOptions.longevity: Best option when turbo failure caused secondary damage to pistons or bearings

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is caught early with no secondary damage, specialist turbo replacement offers good value. However, if you're seeing blue smoke, metallic knocking or loss of compression, a rebuilt M9R engine from our network provides comprehensive protection against repeat failure - often at a comparable total cost.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt turbo M9R engine prices for your Renault Koleos

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - The Rattle That Can Destroy Koleos Petrol Engines
problems.affectedModels: Renault Koleos Authentique 2.0, Expression 2.0, Dynamique 2.0 (2008-2015, M4R 700/702/704)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling on cold starts and potential valve-piston contact if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain, tensioner and guides replaced before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if caught early; prevents engine destruction

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head repair if valves bent but block undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if compression test confirms no piston damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components and oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when chain failure caused internal damage; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold starts from your Koleos petrol engine, act immediately - a preventative timing chain service is far cheaper than repairing valve damage. If the engine has already suffered internal damage, compare quotes for a rebuilt M4R unit; our network's upgraded components address the original tensioner weakness.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt M4R engine replacement for your Renault Koleos

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Hard Starting and Power Loss on Koleos dCi
problems.affectedModels: Renault Koleos all dCi variants (2008-2020, M9R series with common rail injection)
problems.rootCause: HPFP internal wear reduces fuel pressure, causing hard starting, rough running, and eventual no-start conditions; metal debris can damage injectors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pump recalibration or seal replacement if internal wear minimal
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for early-stage faults; not for metal contamination

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished pump fitted with updated fuel lines and filters
repairOptions.longevity: Restores proper fuel pressure; 50k+ mile expected lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded fuel system components
repairOptions.longevity: Best option when HPFP failure contaminated injectors or damaged cylinders

problems.recommendation: For isolated HPFP faults on Koleos under 100k miles, specialist pump replacement offers good value. If diagnostic tests show injector damage or cylinder scoring from metal debris, compare quotes for a full rebuilt M9R engine - our network's units include upgraded fuel system components to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R engine prices with upgraded fuel system for your Renault Koleos

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Koleos
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing DPF warnings, turbo noise, timing chain rattle or fuel pump issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Koleos specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Koleos Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Koleos and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Renault Koleos 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L dCi diesel is the backbone of the Koleos range, powering most UK models from 2008-2020 with strong torque for towing and motorway cruising, though DPF sensitivity requires regular longer journeys.

items.title: Renault Koleos 2.0L Petrol Engines

commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle
oil consumption increase on higher-mileage units

items.title: Renault Koleos 2.5L Petrol Engines

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Koleos Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Koleos engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early M9R dCi to latest Blue dCi variants.

items.title: Renault Koleos Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L M9R dCi diesel dominates Koleos replacement enquiries due to high UK fleet usage and predictable DPF/EGR failure patterns on high-mileage SUVs.
items.descriptor: The 2.0L M9R dCi diesel dominates Koleos replacement enquiries due to high UK fleet usage and predictable DPF/EGR failure patterns on high-mileage SUVs.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Koleos Diesel Engine Families
families: M9R 700/704 - 150/175hp Phase 1 units; DPF sensitivity on urban-driven vehicles
M9R 830/868 - Updated Phase 2 with Blue dCi emissions; higher complexity, similar failure modes
R9N 401 - 1.8L Blue dCi late-model; limited UK fitment, specialist sourcing required
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and motorway cruising
DPF regeneration failure on frequent short-journey usage
EGR valve clogging causing limp mode after 80k+ miles
High UK replacement demand on Dynamique and Privilege dCi variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Koleos Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Koleos Petrol Engines
items.description: M4R and 2QR petrol engines serve lower-mileage Koleos owners seeking simpler emissions compliance, with timing chain and ignition issues driving replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: M4R and 2QR petrol engines serve lower-mileage Koleos owners seeking simpler emissions compliance, with timing chain and ignition issues driving replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Koleos Petrol Engine Families
families: M4R 700/704 - 2.0L naturally aspirated; timing chain tensioner wear after 80k miles
2QR 700 - 2.5L V6 performance option; coolant leaks and ignition faults on aged units
foundIn: Phase 2 (HZJ chassis) (2016-2020) - Limited petrol fitment; most UK models diesel
knownFor: Smooth, linear power delivery with simpler emissions hardware than diesel
Timing chain rattling on cold starts indicating tensioner wear
Ignition coil failure causing misfires and rough idle on city-driven examples
Lower replacement volume than diesel but growing demand as Phase 1 models age

engineYears.h2: Renault Koleos Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Koleos production timeline (2008-2020), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Phase 1 Launch: M9R Diesel & M4R/2QR Petrol Debut
years.description: Launch-year Koleos introduced with 2.0L dCi diesel and two petrol options; early units show higher failure rates due to initial production tolerances.
keyChanges: Koleos launched in UK with Phase 1 (HZG) chassis and Nissan X-Trail platform sharing
M9R 700/704 2.0L dCi introduced as primary engine; M4R 2.0L and 2QR 2.5L petrol options available
Initial DPF and EGR calibration issues identified on early production units
knownFor: Strong initial sales among fleet and towing enthusiasts
Early DPF regeneration issues on urban-driven examples
Timing chain noise reports on M4R petrol units after 60k miles
High parts compatibility with Nissan X-Trail simplifies sourcing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2010 Renault Koleos replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Koleos 2008 engine replacement • M9R 700 DPF failure fix • Koleos timing chain rattle repair

years.preview: Phase 1 Facelift: M9R 830 Refinement & Emissions Updates
years.description: Mid-cycle update introduced revised M9R 830 with improved emissions hardware; DPF sensitivity remained a key concern for UK urban drivers.
keyChanges: M9R 830 replaced earlier codes with updated EGR and DPF calibration
Bose Edition and Initiale Paris trims added with enhanced equipment
Euro 5 compliance introduced for new registrations from 2011
knownFor: Improved cold-start refinement on updated M9R units
Persistent DPF warning lights on short-journey usage patterns
Growing replacement demand as first-generation models approached 100k miles
Strong residual values for well-maintained 4x4 diesel examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2015 Renault Koleos replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Koleos 2013 engine replacement • M9R 830 EGR valve fix • Koleos DPF regeneration service

years.preview: Phase 2 Reintroduction: Blue dCi & Euro 6 Compliance
years.description: Koleos returned to UK with all-new Phase 2 (HZJ) platform; M9R 868 Blue dCi introduced with complex aftertreatment systems raising repair costs.
keyChanges: Completely redesigned HZJ chassis with enhanced NVH and safety features
M9R 868 Blue dCi introduced with SCR/AdBlue system for Euro 6 compliance
GT Line and Initiale Paris trims became primary UK offerings; petrol options discontinued
knownFor: Significant improvement in refinement and interior quality over Phase 1
AdBlue system faults and SCR catalyst issues driving replacement enquiries
Higher complexity of Blue dCi systems increasing specialist repair costs
Lower UK sales volume than Phase 1 but strong demand among existing owners
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2020 Renault Koleos replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Koleos 2018 engine replacement • M9R 868 AdBlue system fault • Koleos Blue dCi SCR catalyst replacement
enquiries: Koleos Blue dCi SCR catalyst replacement

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Renault Koleos was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Renault Koleos specialists.

faq.h2: Renault Koleos Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Koleos engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Renault Koleos engine prices range from £1,400-£2,800 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Supply & fit adds £600-£900 labour.
keyPoints: M9R 2.0L diesel rebuilt: £1,400-£2,400 supply-only
M4R 2.0L petrol rebuilt: £1,600-£2,600 supply-only
2.5L petrol units command premium pricing due to lower availability
Always request itemised quotes including warranty terms
items.cta: Compare Renault Koleos engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Renault Koleos?
items.answer: Main UK Koleos engine codes are M9R series (2.0L diesel), M4R (2.0L petrol) and 2QR (2.5L petrol); verify via VIN or registration lookup before ordering.
keyPoints: M9R 700/704/830/868 cover all 2.0L dCi variants 2008-2020
M4R 700/704 power 2.0L petrol models 2008-2015
2QR 700 used in 2.5L petrol Privilege/Signature 2008-2015
Enter registration above for instant code verification
items.cta: Check your Renault Koleos engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Do you supply and fit Renault Koleos engines?
items.answer: Yes - our UK network offers both supply-only and full supply & fit Renault Koleos engine replacement, with nationwide delivery and vetted specialist fitting.
keyPoints: Supply-only: engine delivered to your chosen garage
Supply & fit: complete replacement including labour, fluids and testing
All fitting specialists vetted and rated by real Koleos owners
12-month warranty covers both engine and installation workmanship
items.cta: Find Renault Koleos engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault Koleos recon engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Renault Koleos engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across UK mainland
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options at extra cost
Always confirm exact terms and claim process before ordering
items.cta: Compare Renault Koleos recon engine warranty options

items.question: How long does Renault Koleos engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 working days for engine supply, plus 1-2 days for fitting; urgent next-day options available for common M9R codes.
keyPoints: Common M9R diesel codes often available for next-day dispatch
Petrol and rare codes may require 5-7 day sourcing time
Professional fitting typically completes in 1-2 working days
Total timeline: 4-7 days from order to road-ready
items.cta: Get urgent Renault Koleos engine replacement quotes today

items.question: Can I replace my Koleos engine with a different code?
items.answer: Engine code swaps on Renault Koleos require ECU reprogramming and emissions system compatibility checks; we recommend matching original code for hassle-free replacement.
keyPoints: M9R variants generally interchangeable within same generation
Cross-generation swaps need specialist calibration and testing
Emissions compliance (Euro 5/6) must be maintained for MOT
Our specialists advise on viable upgrade paths if requested
items.cta: Speak to a Renault Koleos engine compatibility specialist

items.question: Are used Renault Koleos engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Koleos engines can offer good value if sourced from verified low-mileage donors with compression test reports; always request history before purchase.
keyPoints: Request compression test and leak-down report from supplier
Verify mileage via service history or MOT records
Budget for ancillary replacement (injectors, turbo, DPF) during install
Rebuilt units with warranty often better long-term value
items.cta: Check used Renault Koleos engine availability with history reports

items.question: What is the difference between recon and remanufactured Koleos engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines replace worn parts after inspection; remanufactured units are rebuilt to factory tolerances with new components throughout - premium option for long-term ownership.
keyPoints: Recon: cost-effective, addresses visible wear items
Reman: factory-spec rebuild with new pistons, bearings, seals
Reman typically carries longer warranty options
Choose based on vehicle value and intended ownership period
items.cta: Compare recon vs remanufactured Renault Koleos engine costs

items.question: Do Renault Koleos 4x4 engines cost more to replace?
items.answer: 4x4 Renault Koleos engine replacement may cost £100-£300 more due to additional drivetrain compatibility checks and transfer case verification during installation.
keyPoints: Engine pricing similar but fitting complexity increases labour time
Transfer case and AWD system must be inspected during replacement
M9R 704/868 4x4 codes require specialist calibration post-fit
Always confirm 4x4 experience when selecting fitting specialist
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Koleos 4x4 engine replacement specialists

items.question: How do I find my Renault Koleos engine code?
items.answer: Your Renault Koleos engine code appears on the engine block stamping, V5C registration document, or can be instantly verified by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Check V5C section D.2 for engine capacity and code reference
Engine block stamping visible near timing cover or oil filter housing
Service records and MOT certificates often list engine details
Use our free registration lookup for instant code confirmation
items.cta: Verify your Renault Koleos engine code in seconds
